---
name: data-checklist-django-orm
description: Reusable data-layer performance & quota checklist for Django ORM projects (Postgres / MySQL / SQLite). Generated by the data-optimize skill.
---

# Django ORM Data-Layer Checklist — `{route, action or scope}`

> **Default target metrics (adjust per project):** queries/request ≤ 10 (read paths), ≤ 5 (write paths), payload ≤ 200 KB / response, p95 latency ≤ 500 ms, no `Scan` of any unindexed table > 10K rows in a hot path. Override at the top of the audit report when a flow has known higher legitimate volume.
>
> **No project SLO documented?** Treat the defaults above as **informational targets**, not hard limits — flag borderline values 🟡 (caution) rather than 🔴 (regression). Only mark 🔴 when an SLO is explicitly written down (in `aidd_docs/memory/` or a SRE doc) and the audit measured a breach.
>
> **Scope:** this checklist covers Django ORM (`models.py` + `QuerySet` API) primarily. Raw SQL (`connection.cursor()`), Django Channels (websockets), and DRF endpoints inherit the same pivots. ORM-bypassing code (raw queries, `RunSQL` migrations, bulk operations) is audited at §5/§8 with the SQL pivot reflex.
>
> Fill `🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 / N/A` per item. Add `file:line` references when fixing.

## 0. Pre-flight

- [ ] **Primary deterministic counters** defined BEFORE coding: queries/request, payload bytes, requests/route, slow-query count (> 100 ms). These are the load-bearing success signals
- [ ] **3-5 baseline runs** of the target user-flow captured (django-debug-toolbar overlay, `silk` profiler, or `len(connection.queries)` after `settings.DEBUG=True`) — single-run baseline is unfalsifiable
- [ ] **Baseline JSON persisted** at `aidd_docs/tasks/audits/baselines/<scope-slug>.json` with `{ date, runs[], median, p95, queries, payload_bytes }` — enables cross-run comparison without re-baselining
- [ ] Latency p50/p95/p99 captured (server logs, APM, or `wrk -d 30s` against a representative route)
- [ ] **Cold vs warm**: gunicorn worker boot ≠ steady-state. Restart workers, fire 1 request, then 5 — report cold/warm separately
- [ ] PostgreSQL `pg_stat_statements` enabled (`shared_preload_libraries`); `SELECT * FROM pg_stat_statements ORDER BY total_exec_time DESC LIMIT 20` baseline noted
- [ ] Acceptance threshold defined per metric BEFORE coding — primary deterministic delta + secondary p95 latency, with explicit "if latency variance dominates, trust deterministic delta" fallback

## 1. Query patterns (N+1, eager-load, batch)

- [ ] Every queryset traversing a FK / OneToOne uses `select_related('fk1', 'fk2__nested')` — every reverse-FK / M2M uses `prefetch_related('m2m', Prefetch('reverse', queryset=...))`. Missing one = N+1 guaranteed
- [ ] No queryset access inside a `for` loop without prior `select_related` / `prefetch_related` (anti-pattern: `for obj in qs: obj.fk.field` → 1 query per iteration)
- [ ] Service-layer helpers (`build_<entity>_queryset`) centralize joins — avoid repeating `select_related` patterns across views
- [ ] Counts via `qs.count()` (single SQL `COUNT`), never `len(qs)` on hydrated queryset (full materialization)
- [ ] Existence checks via `qs.exists()`, never `if list(qs):` or `if qs.count() > 0:` for boolean tests
- [ ] Aggregations via `.aggregate(...)` / `.annotate(...)` — pushed to SQL, not Python loops
- [ ] Batch updates via `qs.update(field=value)` (single SQL UPDATE) — never `for obj in qs: obj.field = value; obj.save()`
- [ ] Batch creates via `Model.objects.bulk_create([...], batch_size=500)` — never `for x in xs: Model.objects.create(...)`
- [ ] `Model.objects.in_bulk(ids)` for ID-keyed dict lookup over multiple loops
- [ ] `only(...)` / `defer(...)` applied where heavy fields (TextField, JSONField with large blobs) are not displayed
- [ ] django-debug-toolbar (dev) or `silk` (staging) installed — query count visible per request

## 2. Pagination & limits

- [ ] Lists > 50 items paginate via `Paginator` (offset-based, OK on small/medium tables) — or DRF `CursorPagination` for large datasets / append-only feeds
- [ ] Hard cap enforced server-side: queryset never returns full table (slice `[:N]` or paginate)
- [ ] No `.all()` followed by `[start:end]` on huge tables — use `Paginator.get_page(n)` (handles bounds + count) or cursor pagination
- [ ] Search / filter views: `qs.filter(...)` then paginate — never `list(qs)` then Python slicing
- [ ] `Paginator.count` is itself a `COUNT(*)` query — for tables > 1M rows, replace with cached count or `EXPLAIN`-based estimate
- [ ] Infinite scroll endpoints use cursor (`?after=<id>`) not offset (offset stress on `OFFSET 100000` is real)

## 3. Real-time / change streams (Django Channels, signals)

- [ ] `post_save` / `pre_save` signals don't trigger heavy queries on every save — heavy work goes to Celery (or `transaction.on_commit`)
- [ ] Signal handlers idempotent — receiving same signal twice (test setup, fixtures) doesn't double-write
- [ ] Django Channels group `channel_layer.group_send` payload bounded — no full queryset serialization
- [ ] Websocket consumers (`AsyncJsonWebsocketConsumer`) close on disconnect — no leaked DB connections (`database_sync_to_async` blocks pool)
- [ ] Long-polling endpoints capped (max wait ≤ 30 s) — gunicorn workers saturable
- [ ] No SSE / SignalR-style fan-out of a hot table without filter (`Document.objects.all()` streamed = quota burn)

## 4. Caching layer

- [ ] `@cache_page(seconds)` decorator on hot read views with stable content (anonymous browse pages, public profiles)
- [ ] `cache.get_or_set(key, callable, timeout)` for expensive aggregations (homepage stats, tag clouds, distinct-tag lists)
- [ ] Template fragment caching `{% cache timeout fragment_name var1 %}` for repeated UI blocks (sidebars, navigations)
- [ ] Cache backend: Redis / Memcached in prod (NOT `LocMemCache` cross-worker — each gunicorn worker has its own copy)
- [ ] Cache invalidation strategy explicit: TTL OR signal-based (`post_save → cache.delete(key)`) — never silent staleness
- [ ] **Versioned cache keys** for schema-affecting changes (`v2:tags:distinct`) — avoids stale data after migration
- [ ] HTTP cache headers: `Cache-Control: public, max-age=...` on static pages; `Vary: Cookie` on user-specific responses
- [ ] CDN cache (Cloudflare, Fastly): `s-maxage` for edge cache, separate from `max-age` (browser)
- [ ] No `cache.set(key, queryset)` — pickling a lazy queryset persists the SQL, not results. Always `list(qs)` or `.values()` first

## 5. Payload optimization

- [ ] DRF / JSON responses use `serializer = ...(qs, many=True)` with `fields = [...]` whitelist — never `__all__` exposing internal fields
- [ ] HTML responses: heavy fields (`description: TextField`) excluded from list views via `only(...)`, included only on detail pages
- [ ] gzip / brotli enabled at reverse-proxy (nginx, Caddy) — verify via `curl -I` `content-encoding` header
- [ ] No `JsonResponse({'data': list(qs.values())})` returning > 1 MB — paginate or stream
- [ ] Image fields stored as URLs (`ImageField` returning `.url`), not base64 in response
- [ ] `JSONField` payload < 50 KB / row — split denormalized blobs into related model if larger

## 6. Quota & cost awareness

- [ ] PostgreSQL `max_connections` ≥ `gunicorn_workers × DATABASES['default']['CONN_MAX_AGE']` headroom — CONN_MAX_AGE > 0 keeps persistent connections; without pgbouncer, set explicitly
- [ ] pgbouncer (or built-in pooler) in front of Postgres if > 20 gunicorn workers — Postgres connection cost ≈ 10 MB / connection
- [ ] gunicorn workers = `2 × cores + 1` (default formula); async views need `--worker-class=uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker`
- [ ] Disk usage tracked: `pg_database_size()` per DB; alert at 80% volume
- [ ] Egress costs (managed Postgres, RDS): payload audit (`§5`) directly drives bill
- [ ] Celery worker pool sized: `--concurrency=N` matches CPU-bound vs I/O-bound mix; idle worker cost = RAM only

## 7. Security & access control

- [ ] Permission checks via `@login_required` / `@permission_required` / DRF `permission_classes` — not via `if request.user.is_authenticated:` scattered in view bodies
- [ ] No `User.objects.get(pk=request.user.pk)` re-fetch in views — `request.user` is already hydrated
- [ ] Querysets filtered by ownership BEFORE `.get()`: `MyModel.objects.filter(owner=request.user).get(pk=pk)` — not `MyModel.objects.get(pk=pk)` then check
- [ ] No raw SQL with f-string interpolation — always parameterized (`cursor.execute("... WHERE x = %s", [value])`)
- [ ] CSRF middleware enabled; `@csrf_exempt` justified inline if used (only safe for stateless API endpoints with token auth)
- [ ] Sensitive fields (`password_hash`, internal flags) excluded from default serializers
- [ ] `SECRET_KEY` / DB credentials from env vars, never committed (`config/settings/base.py` reads from `os.environ`)

## 8. Schema & indexing

- [ ] All FK columns indexed (Django adds index automatically on `ForeignKey` — verify with `manage.py sqlmigrate`)
- [ ] Filtered columns explicitly indexed: `Meta.indexes = [models.Index(fields=['status', 'created_at'])]` — composite where filtered together
- [ ] `unique=True` / `unique_together` translates to UNIQUE INDEX — leverage for fast lookups
- [ ] Slow queries (`pg_stat_statements`) systematically reviewed; missing index proposed via `EXPLAIN ANALYZE`
- [ ] No `ORDER BY` on un-indexed column for large tables (Postgres falls back to disk sort)
- [ ] `db_index=True` on individual fields heavily filtered alone; otherwise prefer composite `Meta.indexes`
- [ ] Migrations reviewed before merge: `manage.py sqlmigrate <app> <num>` shows generated SQL — flag `ALTER TABLE` blocking on prod-sized tables (use `RunPython` + `concurrent_index` pattern)
- [ ] Postgres-specific: GIN indexes on `JSONField` paths queried; `pg_trgm` for `icontains` search; full-text via `SearchVector` + indexed `GeneratedField`

## 9. Background jobs & async

- [ ] Long tasks (> 1 s) offloaded to Celery / `django-q2` — never block the request
- [ ] Celery tasks idempotent — retry doesn't double-write (use `task_id` deduplication or DB unique constraint)
- [ ] `transaction.on_commit(lambda: send_task.delay(...))` — fire Celery only after DB commit, never inside transaction
- [ ] Task signatures: pass primitive types (IDs), not full ORM objects — pickling a model loses freshness
- [ ] Retry policy explicit: `autoretry_for=(Exception,), retry_backoff=True, max_retries=3` — no infinite loops
- [ ] Dead-letter queue or alerting on Celery failures (Flower / Sentry / Prometheus exporter)
- [ ] Schedule (Celery Beat / `django-q2`): no overlapping schedules of the same heavy task — use `acks_late` + lock
- [ ] **Fallback EAGER mode** (small deploy without Celery worker): tasks run synchronously — audit `CELERY_TASK_ALWAYS_EAGER=True` impact on request latency

## 10. Verification & non-regression

- [ ] PostgreSQL `pg_stat_statements`: top 20 by `total_exec_time` reviewed; recurring slow queries indexed or cached
- [ ] django-debug-toolbar in dev (`INTERNAL_IPS = ['127.0.0.1']`) — every developer sees query count per page
- [ ] `silk` in staging (`SILKY_PYTHON_PROFILER=True`) — non-regression on hot routes
- [ ] CI smoke: `wrk -d 30s -c 10 http://localhost:8000/<hot-route>` — RPS baseline tracked over time
- [ ] Sentry / OpenTelemetry: APM on prod with N+1 detector enabled (Sentry has built-in)
- [ ] Slow query log on Postgres (`log_min_duration_statement = 100`) — alerts on regressions
- [ ] Synthetic test: assert query count via `assertNumQueries(N)` on hot views — guards against silent N+1 regressions

## 11. Checklist self-audit (feedback loop)

Walk through after every audit:

- [ ] Did I find an issue NOT covered by any bullet above? → `[gap] §N: <missing bullet>` in audit report
- [ ] Did a bullet apply but trigger a false positive on this stack/project? → `[fp] §N: <bullet> — reason`
- [ ] Was a bullet ambiguous? Reword in place: `[reword] §N: <before> → <after>`
- [ ] Did I find a recurring anti-pattern across ≥ 2 occurrences? → `[antipattern] <pattern> | <why rejected>`
- [ ] Did I use a useful ad-hoc grep / SQL command? → `[grep] <command> — <what it surfaces>`
- [ ] Was a pivot missing in `references/api-mapping.md` for this stack? → `[pivot] django-orm: <missing pivot>`
- [ ] Was the unit of work ambiguous (queries vs requests vs HTTP roundtrips)? → `[unit] §N: <ambiguity> → <wording>`
- [ ] Did I find a single-occurrence bug? → roadmap entry + tracker issue, NOT into `[antipattern]`. Threshold for elevation: ≥ 2 distinct occurrences OR a recognized generic class

## Common anti-patterns (rejected)

| Pattern | Pourquoi rejeté | Alternative |
|---|---|---|
| `for obj in qs: obj.fk.field` sans `select_related` | N+1 garanti — 1 query par itération | `qs.select_related('fk')` puis loop |
| `len(qs)` ou `list(qs)` pour compter | Hydrate tous les objets en mémoire | `qs.count()` (SQL `COUNT`) |
| `Model.objects.get(pk=pk)` puis check ownership | Lookup même si l'utilisateur n'a pas le droit | `Model.objects.filter(owner=request.user).get(pk=pk)` |
| `cache.set(key, qs)` sur un lazy queryset | Pickle la SQL, pas le résultat | `cache.set(key, list(qs))` ou `.values()` |
| Signal `post_save` exécutant work lourd inline | Bloque chaque save() — gélifie l'admin | `transaction.on_commit(lambda: heavy_task.delay(pk))` |
| `Paginator.count` sur table > 1M rows | `COUNT(*)` plein scan à chaque page | Cached count OR `EXPLAIN`-based estimate OR cursor pagination |
| `LocMemCache` en prod multi-worker | Cache par worker, hit-rate ≈ 1/N | Redis / Memcached partagé |
| Celery task acceptant un objet ORM | Pickle dataclass figée, pas refresh | Passer l'ID, refetch dans la task |

## Quick verification commands

```bash
# Compter SQL queries / page (dev)
# Activer django-debug-toolbar puis observer le panneau SQL — ou en script :
python manage.py shell -c "
from django.test.utils import CaptureQueriesContext
from django.db import connection
from django.test.client import Client
c = Client()
with CaptureQueriesContext(connection) as ctx:
    c.get('/explorer/')
print(f'queries: {len(ctx)}')
for q in ctx[:5]: print(q['sql'][:120])
"

# Détecter les loops sans select_related/prefetch_related
rg -n "for .+ in .+:" --type py -A 3 | rg -B 1 "\.objects\.|\.filter\(" | rg -v "select_related|prefetch_related|values\(|exists\(|count\("

# Détecter `len(qs)` au lieu de `qs.count()`
rg -n "len\([a-z_]+\.objects\.|len\([a-z_]+\.filter\(" --type py

# Détecter les `cache.set` avec un queryset (anti-pattern pickle)
rg -n "cache\.set\([^,]+,\s*[A-Z][a-z]+\.objects\." --type py

# Lister les indexes Postgres existants pour une table
psql -c "SELECT indexname, indexdef FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename = '<table>'"

# Top 20 slow queries (pg_stat_statements doit être enabled)
psql -c "SELECT substring(query, 1, 80) AS query, calls, total_exec_time::int, mean_exec_time::int FROM pg_stat_statements ORDER BY total_exec_time DESC LIMIT 20"

# Vérifier les vues sans @cache_page sur routes anonymes
rg -n "^def \w+\(request" --type py -B 2 | rg -v "cache_page|login_required" | head -20

# Détecter les Celery tasks acceptant un ORM object
rg -n "@(shared_task|app\.task)" --type py -A 2 | rg "def \w+\(.*: [A-Z][a-z]+[,)]"
```
